era_context: The Middle Bronze Age in the Eastern Mediterranean and Near East. The Old Babylonian period under Samsu-iluna; Egypt in the Second Intermediate Period; the Hittite Old Kingdom; Mycenaean Greece in Early Bronze Age III; the Indus Valley Civilization in its mature phase.

events.title: Continued reign of Samsu-iluna in Babylon
events.description: Samsu-iluna continued his reign as king of Babylon during this period. His rule was characterized by efforts to maintain Babylonian territorial control against external pressures and internal fragmentation. The year -1759 falls within the middle decades of his reign, a period of gradual decline in Babylonian power relative to the height achieved under his father Hammurabi.

sources.name: Babylonian king lists and cuneiform administrative texts

events.certainty_note: Samsu-iluna's reign is attested in cuneiform sources, but the specific events of -1759 are not individually documented. The dating of this year depends on the chronology of the Old Babylonian period, which remains debated among scholars.

disconfirming_evidence: The absolute chronology of the Old Babylonian period is contested. Some scholars advocate for a 'low chronology' that would place Samsu-iluna's reign 50–150 years later than the conventional chronology used here. Additionally, no specific events are documented for -1759 in surviving sources; this entry reflects the general political context of Samsu-iluna's reign rather than documented events for this specific year.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1759 is extremely sparse. Cuneiform sources provide administrative and legal texts but do not typically date events to specific years in a way that modern scholars can verify. The chronology of the Old Babylonian period remains one of the most debated issues in ancient Near Eastern chronology, with significant implications for the dating of all events in this period. The surviving sources are heavily weighted toward Mesopotamian administrative and legal perspectives; the broader historical context of other regions is largely invisible.

graph_edges.note: Both entries refer to Samsu-iluna's reign in Babylon
